workspace-tooling.description: Shared repo-root build/lint/type-check/formatting config — not owned by any single package or app.

ci-pipeline.description: Automated CI gate — GitHub Actions running lint/typecheck/test on every PR and push. Distinct from workspace-tooling (the config it invokes) and test-infrastructure (what the tests exercise).

design-spec.description: Design specification — decisions, design contracts, epic specs before implementation. Not the implementation itself, but the approved blueprint.

test-infrastructure.description: Test setup, runners, fixtures, and seeding used to exercise a unit — vitest config, mock data, scenario seeds, Playwright-BDD e2e specs.

package-scaffold.description: Initial package/app skeleton — manifest, tsconfig, barrel exports, directory layout.

type-definitions.description: Internal type/interface declarations and config-shape plumbing — engine-owned types with no runtime domain behavior. Low graph-knowledge weight; typically blacklisted.

archive-structure.description: The two-axis knowledge archive — index.json, schema, append/check tooling, compaction rules.

database-schema.description: SQLite storage substrate — Drizzle schema tables, db connection, schema init, migrations.

data-access.description: Store ports and their SQLite implementations — query/update operations over the schema (LearningStore, ContentStore, ReviewStore, AnswerEventStore, …).

logging.description: Structured logging port and implementations (Noop/Pino), context and correlation.

api-contract.description: HTTP wire-format types shared across client and server — response envelope, error codes, zod schemas.

http-server.description: Backend HTTP API surface — Hono app, routes, middleware, Workers/node runtime. Distinct from a frontend SPA's client-side navigation (see client-routing) — same word, different aspect.

client-routing.description: Frontend SPA navigation — Vue Router instance, route-name constants, and navigation guards (confirm-on-leave, curation-only gating). Distinct from backend http-server routing.

spaced-repetition.description: SRS scheduling core — FSRS interval computation, due-date scheduling, review-card state.

review-session.description: Running a review pass — fetching due cards, auto-rating answers, re-scheduling. The app-level review flow, distinct from the scheduling core.

learning-session-lifecycle.description: Adaptive learning-path session orchestration — init/advance, active pool, queue, batch loop.

user-sessions.description: User identity/auth session — request-scoped userId, auth validation.

in-memory-state-store.description: Process-lifetime, pre-persistence server state (word-state array, batch registry) that backed the HTTP API before a real database existed. Superseded by database-schema/data-access; historical, typically blacklisted.

learning-authority.description: Server-authoritative answer handling — MCQ distractor generation and correctness determination, plus the durable per-answer state transition (applyAnswer) with rollout gate, shadow parallel-run, and golden-master parity testing. Distinct from batch-composition (content selection, not answer authority).

batch-composition.description: Assembling quiz batches from the active pool — MCQ + sentence question generation, composer registry. Distinct from batch-serving (runtime play-through) and batch-validation (post-composition integrity check).

batch-serving.description: Per-batch question serving and retry — the runtime play-through state machine that pops the next question and re-enqueues wrong answers. Distinct from batch-composition (building the question set).

batch-validation.description: Pure post-composition integrity checker for a finished batch — a safety net, not a repair mechanism. No domain behavior of its own; typically blacklisted.

mastery-tracking.description: Per-word learning state — streaks, mastery thresholds, graduation, and the recheck flow that suppresses streak/mastery on a first recheck attempt.

sentence-scheduling.description: Eligibility gating and streak tracking for sentence (word-block) questions — when a sentence context becomes available and how its streak/shelving state evolves. Parallel to word shelving, but sentence-scoped.

shelving.description: Word shelving policy — easy/okay/hard shelf boundaries and distributions.

curriculum.description: Learning content — decks, conversation lines, vocabulary words; curriculum import and curation. Distinct from foundational-content (alphabet/character drills).

deck-overview.description: Deck detail/overview screen — conversation sentences with translations, per-word mastery/shelving status, and navigation into audio playback and sentence-by-sentence learning. Distinct from deck selection (curriculum) and the quiz-play UI itself.

foundational-content.description: Foundational alphabet/character seed data — consonants, vowels, tones, hiragana, katakana — distinct from conversational curriculum content.

language-config.description: Per-language display rules — how words join into a displayed sentence (space vs no-space scripts like Thai, Japanese, Chinese, Korean).

demo-harness.description: Reference terminal-CLI host built on the library's public subpaths — shows how to wire a consuming interface. Not product-facing; typically blacklisted.

web-demo.description: Reference web-SPA demo built on the library's public subpaths — shows how to wire a consuming interface. Not product-facing; typically blacklisted.

audio-timing.description: Audio playback and WebVTT cue timing — wavesurfer control, marker authoring, audio metadata.

debug-tooling.description: Developer/QA debug-trace recording — correlation IDs per served question, buffered appearance events, replay-artifact export.

api-client.description: Frontend thin fetch wrappers over backend HTTP endpoints — typed request/response calls, no reactive state of their own. Distinct from data-access (backend stores) — same shape, different side.

ui-components.description: Vue 3 single-file components — quiz cards, dashboards, audio UI.

ui-state.description: Generic Vue 3 composables holding app-level state/bootstrapping not covered by a more specific ryoiki (e.g. the app boot sequence).
